title: Environment variable to temporarily update container registry resolution
body:
### Feature request description

When executing scripts that use `docker run` (with `podman-is-docker`) or `podman run`, these scripts sometimes assume Docker Hub as the registry and do not play well with ` registries.conf` that is more locked down. Although there are ways to update this (by editing `/etc/containers/registries.conf`), it would be useful to have a lighter-weight solution for one-off cases that do not require permanently modifying one's registry resolution configuration.

### Suggest potential solution

```
$ export FALLBACK_RESOLVE_UNPREFIXED_CONTAINERS=some/container,other/container
$ export FALLBACK_RESOLVE_UNPREFIXED_REGISTRY=docker.io
$ ./do/some/script.sh  # that invokes "podman run ..." with "some/container" and/or "other/container"
```

Basically, provide some environment variables that can be used as a fallback in the resolution process. This would allow the registry resolution changes to be scoped to the current session and not persisted to completely new terminals.


### Have you considered any alternatives?

1. Edit "~/config/containers/registries.conf.d"
   - This does not appear to be supported
   - This would persist longer than intended.

2. Edit "/etc/containers/registries.conf"
  - Changes settings for the whole system / all users on the system
  -  This would persist longer than intended.
  -  Requires additional privileges


### Additional context

This specifically came up in the context of a build script for another project where the `Makefile` references an unprefixed container (from `docker.io`), and where my contributions there are infrequent enough to not merit a permanent addition.

comments.body:
Adding a env var for each option does not scale, we do however allow you to set a custom config path via env var.

So you can already achieve this by writing a tmp registries.conf file with all your options and then setting `CONTAINERS_REGISTRIES_CONF` env to this file path in the session where you want this behaviour.
